job_title: Summer 2026 Intern - Undergrad User Research - Slack
job_description:
• Life at Salesforce Our Locations Our Locations North America Europe, Middle East, and Africa Asia Pacific Latin America Our Teams Our Teams Sales Technology & Product Customer Success Corporate Roles AI Research University University Overview Interns New Grads Advanced Degrees Associate Program Manager Talent Programs Talent Programs Overview Future Pathways India Return to Work Military Fellows Must be graduating December 2026 or later with a Bachelor’s DegreeA strong portfolio, showcasing foundational proficiency in different steps of a research process, such as: qualitative data collection, cleaning and analysis, synthesis, and documenting and sharing findingsCommitment to learning from others that equals a passion for sharing what you’ve learnedSelf-awareness of your strengths and growth areas and a desire to improve your skillsExperience working and collaborating with diverse teams in a dynamic environmentExperience collaborating with others (either research or other functions) to understand, explore, and answer research questionsExcellent written and verbal communication skills Our Story Newsroom Blog Careers Trust Salesforce.
• Org Sustainability Investors Give us your Feedback Sitemap Legal Terms of Service Privacy Information Responsible Disclosure Trust Contact Manage Preferences Experience using multiple research methodologies, such as user interviews, usability testing, surveys, diary studies, co-creation, inferential statistics, etc.
• Experience using a remote / digital tool for research, such as UserTesting.
• University Overview Interns New Grads Advanced Degrees Associate Program Manager Work closely with your mentor and user research leadership to define the details of your projects and contribute to the overall direction of your product areaCollaborate with cross functional partners such as design, engineering, and product management to understand the critical research questions and craft a research approach to best answer themConduct actionable research to help guide the product team’s decision-making and design processSolicit and incorporate feedback on your work from your mentor, other user researchers, and multi-functional team members.
• Com, User Interviews, Qualtrics, Surveymonkey, dscout, Zoom, etc.
